boundarylogic 0.1.0

    Getting Started with Boundary Logic: Phases 1–3 Boundary Logic with Real Clinical Data: The Pima Diabetes Dataset

    Prepare a data frame for boundary logic analysis

    bl_prepare_data.Rd

    Converts a multiclass or binary class column to a numeric 0/1 indicator, selects feature columns, and produces a reproducible train/test split.

    Usage

    bl_prepare_data(
  data,
  class_col,
  target_class = NULL,
  feature_cols = NULL,
  train_fraction = 0.8,
  seed = 121L
)

    Arguments

    data

    A data frame.

    class_col

    Character scalar; name of the column holding class labels.

    target_class

    The value in class_col that should be coded as 1. All other values become 0. If NULL, the column must already be numeric 0/1.

    feature_cols

    Character vector of column names to use as features. If NULL (default), all columns except class_col are used.

    train_fraction

    Numeric in (0, 1); proportion of rows allocated to training. Default 0.8.

    seed

    Integer; random seed for the train/test split. Default 121.

    Value

    A list of class "bl_data" with components:

    train_data

    Data frame of training rows with columns var_names + "class" (numeric 0/1).

    test_data

    Data frame of test rows, same structure.

    var_names

    Character vector of feature column names.

    num_vars

    Integer; number of features.

    target_class

    The value that was mapped to 1, or NULL if no conversion was applied.

    Multiclass to binary conversion

    If your data has more than two classes (e.g., datasets::iris has three Species), supply target_class to specify which class maps to 1. All other classes map to 0. For example:

    # datasets::iris: treat "versicolor" as the positive class
bl_prepare_data(datasets::iris, class_col = "Species", target_class = "versicolor")

    If target_class = NULL, the class column must already be numeric 0/1.

    Examples

    # Binary class, versicolor vs rest
bl_dat <- bl_prepare_data(datasets::iris,
                           class_col    = "Species",
                           target_class = "versicolor")
table(bl_dat$train_data$class)
#> 
#>  0  1 
#> 79 41

    On this page
